`feature_005` (3-level ordinal) is monotonically associated with shorter `pfs_months`: each higher level corresponds to a shorter mean PFS.
Treating `feature_005` as a categorical predictor still shows level 2 has the shortest PFS vs level 0 reference.

After adjusting for all other features in a multivariable OLS, `feature_012` remains positively associated with `pfs_months` and `feature_006`, `feature_005`, `feature_013`, `feature_028`, `feature_001`, `feature_018`, `feature_004`, `feature_033` remain significantly associated in their univariate-implied directions.
Older `feature_012` (age-like; range 30-90) is associated with LONGER `pfs_months`, which is unusual and suggests selection or biology (older patients may have more indolent disease).
The positive age (`feature_012`) effect persists after adjusting for `feature_005` (stage-like) and major biomarkers (feature_006, feature_013, feature_001, feature_028).

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=+0.7015, p=0.00e+00; older patients have longer PFS, opposite of typical age effect on overall survival.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_012 + feature_005 + feature_006 + feature_013 + feature_001 + feature_028
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted age coef = +0.17574 mo/yr, p=0.00e+00; persists.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Binary features `feature_029` and `feature_023` are highly co-occurring (Pearson r > 0.5) — consistent with a hormone-receptor pair (e.g., ER and PR positivity), and both are individually associated with LONGER PFS.
After mutual adjustment, `feature_029` and `feature_023` show whether one is the dominant predictor of `pfs_months`.

analyses.result_summary: Co-occurrence r=+0.669, p=0.00e+00. PFS diff: feature_029 +0.568 mo (p=3.70e-143); feature_023 +0.362 mo (p=5.82e-59).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Binary features `feature_018` and `feature_016` are negatively associated (Pearson r < 0), consistent with mutually-exclusive treatment options or alternative biomarker states; their PFS effects move in opposite directions.
`feature_006` (binary, ~30% prevalence) is associated with substantially SHORTER `pfs_months` — likely an aggressive-disease marker.
`feature_001` (binary, ~10% prevalence) is associated with shorter `pfs_months` even after adjusting for stage (feature_005) and age (feature_012).

analyses.code: t-test and OLS adjusting for stage and age
analyses.result_summary: Univariate diff = -1.5363 mo (p=0.00e+00). Adjusted for feature_005 and feature_012: coef = -1.5491 mo (p=0.00e+00).
feature_001 adjusted coef = -0.9905 mo (p=0.00e+00).

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_013` (binary, ~35% prevalence) is associated with LONGER `pfs_months` and may represent a therapeutic intervention or favorable biomarker (e.g., hormonal therapy or hormone-receptor positive disease).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_013 + feature_005 + feature_012 + feature_006
analyses.result_summary: Univariate diff = +1.0981 mo (p=0.00e+00). Adjusted coef = +1.1031 mo (p=0.00e+00).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous biomarker `feature_004` shows a negative association with `pfs_months` after adjusting for stage (feature_005), age (feature_012), and major binary biomarkers (feature_006, feature_013, feature_001).

Continuous biomarker `feature_024` shows a positive association with `pfs_months` after adjusting for stage (feature_005), age (feature_012), and major binary biomarkers (feature_006, feature_013, feature_001).

Continuous biomarker `feature_033` shows a negative association with `pfs_months` after adjusting for stage (feature_005), age (feature_012), and major binary biomarkers (feature_006, feature_013, feature_001).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_004 + feature_005 + feature_012 + feature_006 + feature_013 + feature_001
analyses.result_summary: feature_004 adjusted coef = -0.07763 per unit (p=0.00e+00).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_024 + feature_005 + feature_012 + feature_006 + feature_013 + feature_001
analyses.result_summary: feature_024 adjusted coef = +0.47625 per unit (p=0.00e+00).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_033 + feature_005 + feature_012 + feature_006 + feature_013 + feature_001
analyses.result_summary: feature_033 adjusted coef = -0.01887 per unit (p=0.00e+00).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Lab-value features (sodium-, potassium-, calcium-like values feature_002/007/010 and other panel features feature_017/025/027/031/034/035/036/037/014/021/003) collectively explain little additional variance in `pfs_months` beyond the strong predictors.

analyses.code: Compare R^2 of OLS with strong predictors only vs strong + labs
analyses.result_summary: R^2 strong-only = 0.7919; R^2 strong + 14 labs = 0.7922. Incremental R^2 = 0.00032.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The protective effect of `feature_013` on `pfs_months` is modified by stage (`feature_005`): the absolute PFS benefit varies across stage levels (interaction p<0.05).
Stratified by stage (feature_005), the within-stratum mean PFS difference between feature_013=1 and feature_013=0 is largest in the favorable-stage stratum (feature_005=0).

analyses.result_summary: feature_013 main = +1.1125; interaction with stage 1 = -0.0216 (p=4.18e-01); interaction with stage 2 = -0.0090 (p=8.09e-01).

analyses.code: stratified t-test of pfs_months by feature_013 within each feature_005 level

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative impact of `feature_006` on `pfs_months` interacts with stage (`feature_005`): absolute PFS deficit is larger in higher stages.

analyses.result_summary: feature_006 main = -1.5903; interaction stage1 = +0.0495 (p=8.24e-02); interaction stage2 = +0.1100 (p=5.86e-03).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of `feature_001` (rare ~10% biomarker) on `pfs_months` is amplified at higher stage.

analyses.result_summary: feature_001 main = -1.0089; interaction stage1 = -0.0085 (p=8.68e-01); interaction stage2 = +0.1408 (p=4.41e-02).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is an interaction between `feature_006` and `feature_013` on `pfs_months`: the protective benefit of feature_013 is reduced (or lost) when feature_006=1.
Within feature_006=1 patients, feature_013 still confers a positive (less negative) mean PFS effect compared to feature_013=0.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_006 * feature_013 + feature_005 + feature_012
analyses.result_summary: feature_006 main = -1.5578; feature_013 main = +1.0957; interaction = +0.0248, p=3.61e-01.

analyses.code: Within feature_006=1, t-test PFS by feature_013
analyses.result_summary: feature_006=1 only: feature_013=1 mean PFS=4.335 (n=5218) vs feature_013=0 mean=3.232 (n=9751); diff=+1.1024, p=1.19e-135.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of `feature_028` on `pfs_months` is modified by age (`feature_012`): the decrement is larger (more negative) in younger patients.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_028 * feature_012 + feature_005 + feature_006 + feature_013
analyses.result_summary: feature_028 main = -0.6515; interaction with feature_012 = +0.00090/yr, p=4.63e-01.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treating `feature_013` as a candidate treatment, an exhaustive screen of interactions feature_013 × {every other binary feature} on `pfs_months` (adjusted for stage and age) reveals at least one significant modifier.
Among continuous modifiers, the strongest feature_013 × continuous interaction on `pfs_months` reveals which continuous biomarker most modifies the feature_013 effect.

analyses.code: Loop OLS pfs_months ~ feature_013 * <each binary> + feature_005 + feature_012
analyses.result_summary: Top-5 modifier-by-feature_013 interactions: feature_013 x feature_029: int=+1.5826, p=0.00e+00; feature_013 x feature_018: int=-1.3797, p=0.00e+00; feature_013 x feature_028: int=-1.6731, p=0.00e+00; feature_013 x feature_023: int=+1.0492, p=1.19e-286; feature_013 x feature_016: int=+0.3974, p=2.85e-42.

analyses.result_summary: Top-5 continuous modifiers: feature_013 x feature_033: int=-0.05363, p=0.00e+00; feature_013 x feature_010: int=-0.09945, p=4.09e-04; feature_013 x feature_024: int=-0.06701, p=1.58e-02; feature_013 x feature_037: int=-0.09999, p=2.50e-02; feature_013 x feature_034: int=-0.00245, p=4.33e-02.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treating `feature_006` as a candidate exposure, an exhaustive screen of interactions feature_006 × {every other binary feature} on `pfs_months` reveals significant modifiers of its effect.

analyses.result_summary: Top-5: feature_006 x feature_015: int=-0.0454, p=1.36e-01; feature_006 x feature_022: int=+0.0424, p=2.21e-01; feature_006 x feature_029: int=+0.0342, p=2.52e-01; feature_006 x feature_008: int=-0.0514, p=2.70e-01; feature_006 x feature_013: int=+0.0248, p=3.61e-01.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An exhaustive screen of interactions `feature_018` × {every other binary feature} on `pfs_months` (adjusted) identifies significant modifiers.

analyses.result_summary: Top-5: feature_018 x feature_013: int=-1.3797, p=0.00e+00; feature_018 x feature_028: int=+0.7134, p=2.25e-79; feature_018 x feature_029: int=-0.6479, p=6.31e-61; feature_018 x feature_016: int=+0.0000, p=2.85e-43; feature_018 x feature_023: int=-0.4432, p=1.40e-31.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An exhaustive screen of `feature_001` × {every other binary} on `pfs_months` identifies modifiers.

analyses.result_summary: Top-5: feature_001 x feature_026: int=+0.2532, p=8.09e-02; feature_001 x feature_032: int=+0.0970, p=2.15e-01; feature_001 x feature_013: int=-0.0521, p=2.57e-01; feature_001 x feature_018: int=+0.0658, p=2.69e-01; feature_001 x feature_023: int=-0.0466, p=3.29e-01.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An exhaustive screen of `feature_028` × {every other binary} on `pfs_months` identifies modifiers; the most negative interaction term names the subgroup where feature_028 is most harmful.

analyses.result_summary: Top-5: feature_028 x feature_013: int=-1.6731, p=0.00e+00; feature_028 x feature_029: int=-0.8392, p=2.53e-156; feature_028 x feature_018: int=+0.7134, p=2.25e-79; feature_028 x feature_023: int=-0.5545, p=2.28e-74; feature_028 x feature_016: int=-0.1909, p=3.76e-10.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After standardizing all features to unit variance, the multivariable OLS model on `pfs_months` ranks the dominant predictors as: feature_012 (age), feature_005 (stage-like), feature_006, feature_013, in that approximate order of standardized effect magnitude.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ z-scored(all features); rank by |coef|

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive `feature_013` -> longer `pfs_months` effect is most strongly modified by `feature_029` (interaction p=0.00e+00). Specifically, the within-stratum PFS gain from feature_013=1 is largest when `feature_029`=1.
Within stage 0 patients (feature_005=0), the feature_013 PFS benefit is concentrated where `feature_029`=1 and is approximately +1 month or more.

analyses.code: Stratify by feature_029 and t-test PFS by feature_013
In stage 0 subset, stratify by feature_029 and t-test PFS by feature_013

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of `feature_006` on `pfs_months` is most strongly modified by `feature_015` (interaction p=1.36e-01); the largest absolute decrement occurs in `feature_015`=1.
Within high-stage patients (feature_005=2), the feature_006-associated PFS deficit is compounded when `feature_015`=1 — defining a complete unfavorable subgroup.

analyses.code: Stratify by feature_015 and t-test PFS by feature_006
analyses.result_summary: feature_015=0: feature_006 PFS effect=-1.5009 (p=0.00e+00, n1=10532,n0=24433); feature_015=1: feature_006 PFS effect=-1.6189 (p=3.21e-299, n1=4437,n0=10598). Largest absolute deficit in feature_015=1: -1.6189 mo (p=3.21e-299).

analyses.code: In feature_005=2 subset, stratify by feature_015 and t-test PFS by feature_006

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_013` behaves as a treatment whose PFS benefit is jointly modified by three biomarkers: positive predictor `feature_029` (effect concentrated when feature_029=1), and resistance/contraindication markers `feature_018` and `feature_028` (effect abolished when either equals 1). In a single OLS model `pfs_months ~ feature_013*feature_029 + feature_013*feature_018 + feature_013*feature_028 + feature_005 + feature_012`, the three interaction terms are all highly significant while the main effects of feature_029, feature_018, and feature_028 are ~0.

FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP for `feature_013` -> `pfs_months`: Within the subgroup defined by feature_029=1 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_028=0, patients on feature_013=1 have substantially LONGER pfs_months than feature_013=0 (expected mean difference > +2 months). The complete set of variables whose unfavorable values suppress the feature_013 treatment effect is therefore: feature_029=0 OR feature_018=1 OR feature_028=1.

Outside the predictive subgroup (i.e., feature_029=0 OR feature_018=1 OR feature_028=1), feature_013 has approximately ZERO mean effect on pfs_months and the difference is statistically non-significant.
PROGNOSTIC SUBGROUP: Patients with feature_005=2 AND feature_006=1 AND feature_001=1 have the lowest mean pfs_months of any clean subgroup, substantially below the cohort mean of ~4.69 months.

FAVORABLE SUBGROUP: Patients with feature_005=0 AND feature_006=0 AND feature_001=0 AND feature_029=1 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_013=1 have the highest mean pfs_months of any subgroup defined by these features (well above 8 months).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_013*feature_029 + feature_013*feature_018 + feature_013*feature_028 + feature_005 + feature_012
Subset: feature_029==1 AND feature_018==0 AND feature_028==0; t-test pfs_months by feature_013
analyses.result_summary: In subgroup n=18681: feature_013=1 mean PFS=7.209 (n=6571) vs feature_013=0 mean=4.300 (n=12110); diff=+2.9081 mo, p=0.00e+00.

analyses.code: Subset complement; t-test pfs_months by feature_013
analyses.result_summary: In complement subgroup n=31319: feature_013=1 mean PFS=4.319 (n=10857) vs feature_013=0 mean=4.316 (n=20462); diff=+0.0028 mo, p=9.10e-01.

analyses.code: Subset: feature_005==2 AND feature_006==1 AND feature_001==1; compare PFS to rest
analyses.result_summary: Worst-prognosis subgroup n=247, mean PFS=1.537 vs rest n=49753, mean=4.709; diff=-3.1719 mo, p=6.20e-72.

analyses.code: Subset: all favorable factors AND feature_013==1; compare PFS to rest
analyses.result_summary: Best subgroup n=1471, mean PFS=8.698 vs rest n=48529, mean=4.571; diff=+4.1263 mo, p=0.00e+00.
